title: GitHub Copilot SDK reaches general availability
friendlyTitle: GitHub’s Copilot SDK is ready for production use
summary: GitHub announced that the Copilot SDK is generally available, giving developers a stable way to embed Copilot’s agent runtime, tool calling, file edits, streaming and multi-turn sessions into their own applications.
friendlySummary: The Copilot SDK now lets developers build apps that use Copilot’s agent engine, including planning, tools, file edits and ongoing sessions.
whyItMatters: Instead of every team building an agent orchestration layer from scratch, developers can now plug into a production-ready Copilot runtime. That could accelerate internal developer tools, CI assistants and custom coding workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This makes it easier for companies to build custom coding assistants without inventing all the plumbing themselves.
